components.description:
A simple back button that points to the previous page.
```
<back-button-1 />
```
Generic boolean/toggle filter for product listings.

inputs.name: fieldPath
inputs.description: Dot-notated field path, for example `customFields.inStock`.

inputs.name: label
inputs.description: Label displayed beside the toggle.

inputs.name: enabled
inputs.description: Optional initial state of the toggle.

outputs.name: changed
outputs.description: Emits API-ready filters whenever the toggle state changes.

components.description:
DSL cart component used by the webshop storefront.

Responsibilities:
- Reads persisted items from cart storage.
- Resolves product and choice metadata through shop-svc APIs.
- Keeps the browser cart storage in sync with quantity/remove updates.
- Emits analytics (`view_cart`) and local message-bus events (`cart:remove`).

Notes for DSL usage:
- Route-level component (renders cart page content and sets document title).
- Uses `productsLoaded` as the loading guard for templates.
- Uses integer minor-units for prices (for example cents), converts to
  major-units only when sending analytics payloads.

CategoriesLoader is a renderless data loader component for shop categories.
It loads categories from the backend and emits them through the `loaded` output.

```html
<categories-loader (loaded)="categories = $event" />

<loop [data]="categories" let="category">
  <!-- render category -->
</loop>
```

Headless helper component that allows templates to declare product custom field definitions.

Usage:

<custom-field-definition
  definitions='[{"fieldPath":"material","name":"Material","type":"string"}]'
/>

Outcome:
- Enables template authors to declaratively define custom product fields,
  which then appear in the admin product edit form.

inputs.name: definitions
inputs.description:
Custom field definitions to register for products.

Accepts:
- a single definition object
- an array of definition objects
- a JSON string containing either a single definition or an array

Supported definition shape:
- `fieldPath`: unique config path for the custom field
- `name`: optional admin label
- `type`: optional value type (`string`, `number`, `boolean`, `json`)

Invalid or unparsable values are ignored.
Definitions already present in `products.customFieldDefinitions`
are not duplicated.

components.description:
EditableImage is the standard component for in-place editable images.

Use this when the image should be both displayed and replaceable through the editor UI.
In edit mode, authorized users can click the image and swap it without changing template code.
Outside edit mode, it behaves like a normal image display component.

Unlike `ResponsiveImg` and `ResponsiveImgModal`, this component is content-backed:
the rendered image is resolved from `elementId`, so the same `elementId` can be reused
in multiple places to keep those instances synchronized.

If no image has been configured yet, the component falls back to `placeholder`.

Example:
```
<editable-image elementId="mainHeroImage" />
```

Recommended with placeholder:
```
<editable-image
  elementId="mainHeroImage"
  placeholder="https://images.example.com/hero-fallback.jpg"
/>
```

Reusing the same image in multiple locations:
```
<editable-image elementId="brandLogo" width="160" height="160" />
<editable-image elementId="brandLogo" width="48" height="48" />
```

inputs.name: elementId
inputs.description:
Unique content key for this editable image.
The component uses this ID to load and update the stored image asset.

Multiple instances may intentionally share the same `elementId`
to render the same image in different places or sizes.

inputs.name: width
inputs.description: Preferred rendered width used for the default image URL and automatic srcset generation.

inputs.name: height
inputs.description: Preferred rendered height used for the default image URL and proportional srcset generation.

inputs.name: quality
inputs.description: Controls the generated image quality for both `src` and `srcset` candidates.

inputs.name: placeholder
inputs.description:
A fallback image used if no editable image is configured or if the primary source fails to load.
Providing this is a best practice as it ensures:
- Seamless template portability across different environments.
- Transparent sourcing for easier image license verification.

inputs.name: modal
inputs.description: Enables the shared image modal instead of rendering the plain inline image.

inputs.name: sizes
inputs.description:
Responsive slot sizes passed to the browser.
Defaults to `(max-width: {width}px) 100vw, {width}px` when not provided.

inputs.name: widths
inputs.description:
Optional list of candidate widths used to build `srcset`.
Leave undefined for sensible defaults or pass an empty array to disable `srcset` entirely.

inputs.name: loading
inputs.description: Native loading hint forwarded to the underlying ``.

inputs.name: decoding
inputs.description: Native decoding hint forwarded to the underlying ``.

inputs.name: fetchPriority
inputs.description: Native fetch priority hint forwarded to the underlying ``.

inputs.name: styleKey
inputs.description: DEPRECATED. Will be removed.

inputs.name: defaultCss
inputs.description: DEPRECATED. Will be removed.

components.description:
EditableText is the canonical component for all text display across the platform.
It serves as the primary interface for dynamic, user-adjustable text content,
allowing authorized users to modify text values directly through the UI in edit mode.
```
<editable-text elementId="mainHeroTitle" placeholder="This does not really matter" />
```

inputs.name: elementId
inputs.description:
The authoritative identifier for this component.
This ID is mandatory as it functions as the lookup key for the specific text asset data.
Note: Multiple instances can share the same elementId to display and synchronize
the same text content across different parts of the UI.

inputs.name: styleKey
inputs.description: DEPRECATED. Will be removed.

inputs.name: defaultCss
inputs.description: DEPRECATED. Will be removed.

components.description:
Simple informational banner shown on free-tier storefronts.

The component is static and translation-driven, intended for drop-in usage
in DSL layouts wherever the plan/status notice should appear.
Header context loads information into the template to enable you to implement custom headers. ``` ```

Generic multi-select product filter component.

Renders a list of checkbox options and emits a single `in` filter that can
be passed directly to product loading APIs.

```html
<multiselect-filter-1
  fieldPath="customFields.material"
  [options]="['Cotton', 'Wool', 'Linen']"
  (changed)="filters = $event"
/>
```

- When no option is selected, emits an empty array.
- Supports both plain string options and `{ label, value }` objects.
- Styling is controlled through CSS variables defined in the component stylesheet.

inputs.name: fieldPath
inputs.description: Dot path used in the emitted filter.

inputs.name: heading
inputs.description: Heading rendered on top of the component.

inputs.name: options
inputs.description:
Available options.

Accepts either a string list (`['A', 'B']`) or explicit objects
(`[{ label: 'A', value: 'a' }]`).

inputs.name: selected
inputs.description: Preselected values that should start checked.

outputs.name: changed
outputs.description: Emits API-ready filters on every selection change.

components.description:
Customer order history page component.

Loads signed-in user's recent orders, enriches them with product/choice data,
and exposes a "reorder" action that rehydrates the cart from a past order.
A generic numeric range filter component for product listings.

inputs.name: fieldPath
inputs.description: Dot-notated product field path (for example `customFields.width`).

inputs.name: min
inputs.description: Optional initial minimum value displayed in the minimum input.

inputs.name: max
inputs.description: Optional initial maximum value displayed in the maximum input.

inputs.name: heading
inputs.description: Optional visible heading shown above the filter controls.

outputs.name: changed
outputs.description: Emits the current filter list whenever range values change.

components.description:
Checkout orchestrator for the storefront order flow.

It coordinates the multi-step DSL UI (form -> summary -> payment -> success),
loads cart products/choices, prepares order payloads, and places orders using
shop-svc.

ProductLoader is a renderless data loader component for a single shop product.
It resolves the product id from the current route, loads the product from the backend,
and emits it through the `loaded` output.

```html
<product-loader (loaded)="product = $event" />
```

Intended for product detail pages where the route contains a `product ID` param.

Orchestrates the retrieval of product data with Hydration support.

1. Checks `TransferState` for cached data to prevent double-fetching during hydration.
2. Fetches active products from `shopSvcApi` based on the current hostname.
3. Normalizes image dimensions to prevent **Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS)**.
4. Synchronizes results with the `SearchService` for client-side filtering.

inputs.name: eagerCount
inputs.description:
The number of product images to load immediately with high priority.
*

components.description:
ProductsLoader is a renderless data loader component for shop products.
It loads products from the backend and emits them through the `loaded` output.

```html
<products-loader (loaded)="products = $event" />

<loop [data]="products" let="p">
  <!-- render product -->
</loop>
```

If `categoryId` is provided, it only loads products in that category.

inputs.name: categoryId
inputs.description:
Optional category id filter.
When set, only products from that category are loaded.

inputs.name: filters
inputs.description:
Optional list of low-level ShopSvc filters.
These can be produced by dedicated filter components (range, multiselect, etc.).

outputs.name: loaded
outputs.description:
Emits the loaded product list.
Emits an empty array if loading fails.

components.description:
Search input element for product listing pages.

Emits the current text query on each change and when cleared, so parent DSL
containers can filter products reactively.

If is a visibility toggle that shows or hides content based on a specific condition. It is commonly used to handle "empty states," loading indicators, or gated features. ```
No results found.
Results found! <3
 ```

inputs.name: condition
inputs.description:
The primary evaluation criteria.
When true, the children blocks are rendered to the DOM.

inputs.name: not
inputs.description:
Inverts the condition logic.
If true, the component acts as an "Unless" toggle (renders only when condition is false).

inputs.name: childrenBlocks
inputs.description: Ignore this.

components.description:
Loop is used to display a list of items by repeating a template for every entry in a collection. It makes the data for the "current" item available to all components inside it using a custom name. ```
Name: {{ item.name }} Price: {{ item.cost }}
```

inputs.name: data
inputs.description:
The collection of items to iterate over.
Can be an array of objects, strings, or numbers.
If null/undefined, the loop handles it gracefully by rendering nothing.

inputs.name: let
inputs.description:
Name for the current item inside the loop.

Defines the variable name used to reference each item in the template.
Defaults to `"e"` if not specified.

inputs.name: height
inputs.description:
The fixed height of the scroll container.
Setting this value triggers `overflow: hidden` on the host and is
typically required for virtual scrolling to function correctly.

inputs.name: virtualize
inputs.description:
Enables Virtual Scrolling (CDK).
When true, only the items currently visible in the viewport are rendered.
Best used with large datasets and a defined `height`.

components.description:
Block defines a globally accessible UI fragment.
Once defined, this snippet is stored in a shared registry, making it
available for use across different pages or even entirely different
websites within the platform. It serves as the single source of truth
for shared design patterns.
```
<block key="global-footer">
  <footer class="site-footer">© 2024 Platform Inc.</footer>
</block>
```
To display the content of a block anywhere in your layout (even on other pages),
use the `<include />` component with the corresponding key:
```xml
<include src="global-footer" />
```

inputs.name: key
inputs.description: The unique identifier used to reference this snippet globally.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description:
A friendly name for the block.
This title appears in the Snippet Explorer to help others identify the component.

inputs.name: description
inputs.description:
A brief summary of the block's purpose.
This description is displayed in the Snippet Explorer to explain how the component should be used.

inputs.name: publish
inputs.description:
* When enabled, this block is added to the Public Snippet Explorer
(https://rikrol.com/snippets), allowing others to discover and use it.

inputs.name: display
inputs.description:
Controls whether the block is visible at the location where it is defined.
Set to 'false' if you only want to register the block without rendering it immediately.

components.description:
Include pulls in a shared UI fragment from the global registry.
It can resolve blocks defined locally, on other pages, or from external 
sites, allowing you to build unified interfaces using pre-existing 
components without duplicating code.
```
* <include src="shared-nav-menu" />
```
To include a block from a different website or project, prefix the source 
with the target application ID followed by a colon:
```xml
<include src="app-1234:footer" />
```

inputs.name: src
inputs.description:
Format: "appId:templateId" or just "templateId" (local)
Example: "system:navbar" or "my-custom-footer"

components.description:
Let is the state-initialization component for the DSL.
It allows developers to explicitly declare and initialize variables.
```
<let name="selectedCategory" value="'all'" />
<let name="itemCount" value="0" />
<let name="myArray" value="[{'id': 1}, {'id': 2}]" />
```

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: The variable name the let statement sets

inputs.name: value
inputs.description:
The value to be assigned to the variable.
This input supports:
- **Literals**: Numbers (100) or Strings ('hello' or "world").
- **Variable References**: Unquoted keys (e.g., `user.name`) are resolved to variables.
- **Concatenation**: Combine values using `+` (e.g., `'Hello ' + firstName`).
- **Comparisons**: Evaluates to boolean using `==` or `!=`.
* *Note: While the DSL evaluates strings, you can also pass raw JS Objects/Arrays
directly via Angular property binding [value]="{id: 1}".*

components.description:
ResponsiveImg is the standard display-only component for responsive images.

Use this when an image should be rendered with generated `src` / `srcset`
but should not be editable through the CMS or editor UI.

It supports responsive sizing, loading hints, fallback placeholders,
and common layout controls such as width, height, and object fit.

Example:
```
<responsive-img
  src="/upload/gallery-1.jpg"
  alt="Gallery image"
/>
```

inputs.name: src
inputs.description:
Source URL of the image.
Required input. Can be absolute or relative path, or file ID.

inputs.name: placeholder
inputs.description:
A fallback image used if no editable image is configured or if the primary source fails to load.
Providing this is a best practice as it ensures:
- Seamless template portability across different environments.
- Transparent sourcing for easier image license verification.

inputs.name: alt
inputs.description:
Alternative text for accessibility.
Defaults to empty string.

inputs.name: sizes
inputs.description:
Value for the `sizes` attribute.
Defines how the image should scale across viewport widths.
Defaults to `"100vw"`.

inputs.name: quality
inputs.description:
Image quality (typically 1–100).
Passed to the responsive image service.
Defaults to `80`.

inputs.name: widths
inputs.description:
Array of widths used to generate responsive `srcset`.
Defaults to predefined breakpoints.

inputs.name: loading
inputs.description:
Loading behavior of the image.
`"lazy"` defers loading, `"eager"` loads immediately.

inputs.name: decoding
inputs.description:
Decoding hint for the browser.
`"async"` (default), `"sync"`, or `"auto"`.

inputs.name: fetchPriority
inputs.description:
Fetch priority hint for the browser.
`"high"`, `"low"`, or `"auto"`.

inputs.name: width
inputs.description:
Explicit width of the image.
Can be a number (pixels) or any valid CSS size string.

inputs.name: height
inputs.description:
Explicit height of the image.
Can be a number (pixels) or any valid CSS size string.

inputs.name: maxWidth
inputs.description:
Maximum width constraint.
Accepts number (pixels) or CSS size string.

inputs.name: maxHeight
inputs.description:
Maximum height constraint.
Accepts number (pixels) or CSS size string.

inputs.name: objectFit
inputs.description:
CSS `object-fit` value applied to the image.
Example: `"cover"`, `"contain"`, `"fill"`, etc.

components.description:
ResponsiveImgModal is a display-only image component with lightbox behavior.

Use this when an image should render inline as a responsive preview
and open into an interactive modal when clicked.

The modal supports zooming, rotation, and panning.
This component does not provide in-place editing.

Example:
```
<responsive-img-modal
  src="/upload/gallery-1.jpg"
  alt="Gallery image"
/>
```

inputs.name: src
inputs.description:
Source URL of the image.
Required input. Can be absolute or relative path, or file id.

inputs.name: alt
inputs.description:
Alternative text for accessibility.
Defaults to empty string.

inputs.name: width
inputs.description:
Explicit width of the thumbnail image.
Accepts number (pixels) or any valid CSS size string.

inputs.name: maxWidth
inputs.description:
Maximum width constraint for the thumbnail or container.
Accepts any valid CSS size string.

inputs.name: maxHeight
inputs.description:
Maximum height constraint.
Accepts number (pixels) or CSS size string.

inputs.name: fitContainer
inputs.description:
Determines how the thumbnail fits within its container.
`true` uses "contain", `false` uses "cover".
Defaults to `true`.

inputs.name: sizes
inputs.description:
Value for the `sizes` attribute of the responsive image.
Controls how the browser selects appropriate image sizes.
Defaults to `"100vw"`.

inputs.name: quality
inputs.description:
Image quality parameter (typically 1–100).
Passed to image processing services.
Defaults to `80`.

inputs.name: thumbnailWidths
inputs.description:
Width breakpoints used to generate the responsive thumbnail `srcset`.
Defaults to predefined thumbnail widths.

fields.name: categoryIds
fields.description: References to categories this product belongs to

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: Timestamp when the product was created.

fields.name: customFields
fields.description: Arbitrary JSON-like data for custom product fields.

fields.name: labels
fields.description:
Structured flags or metadata
Arbitrary string key-value labels (e.g. "coupon": "true", "category": "promo")

fields.name: prices
fields.description:
Prices of the product.
Can be multiple (like USD and Credits).

fields.name: sku
fields.description:
Optional SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) identifier.
e.g. "SKU12345", "PROD-001"

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description: Timestamp when the product was last updated.
